IDAHO FINANCIAL ADVISOR

Idaho Retirement Planning with a Trusted Fiduciary Advisor

Capital Wealth serves Idaho families with comprehensive retirement planning. As fiduciaries, we put your interests first — no product sales, no hidden commissions, just honest advice tailored to Idaho's unique tax and retirement landscape.

Schedule Your Visit

Idaho doesn't tax Social Security benefits

CHAPTER I: IDAHO ADVANTAGES

Idaho Has Great Tax Benefits

Idaho is one of the most tax-friendly states for retirees. Social Security benefits aren't taxed, there's a retirement income deduction for residents 65+, and property taxes are reasonable. We help you maximize these advantages.

We understand Idaho's unique opportunities and work with families from Boise to Coeur d'Alene. Whether you're a government retiree, corporate executive, or business owner, we tailor strategies to Idaho's tax code.

  • No Idaho tax on Social Security benefits
  • Retirement income deduction up to $75,600 for 65+
  • Reasonable property taxes compared to other states
  • No inheritance tax
  • Coordination with federal tax strategies
CHAPTER II: FIDUCIARY COMMITMENT

Your Interests Come First

As fee-only fiduciaries, we're legally and ethically bound to put your interests ahead of our own. We don't sell products for commissions or earn kickbacks from mutual funds. Our only compensation comes directly from you for advice and planning.

"A true fiduciary relationship means transparency, trust, and putting your financial success ahead of everything else."
  • Fee-only compensation structure
  • Written fiduciary commitment
  • No commissions or product sales
  • Independent advice (no parent company)
  • Complete transparency in fees and conflicts
CHAPTER III: COMPREHENSIVE PLANNING

Beyond Investments

We don't just manage portfolios. We create comprehensive retirement plans that coordinate income, taxes, Social Security, Medicare, estate planning, and legacy goals. Everything works together to maximize your retirement security.

  • Idaho-specific tax planning and strategies
  • Social Security timing optimization
  • Retirement income and withdrawal planning
  • Medicare coordination and IRMAA management
  • Estate planning and legacy strategies
  • Investment management and rebalancing

Ready for Honest Advice?

Idaho families deserve a financial advisor who puts their interests first. Let's discuss how our fiduciary approach can help you make the most of Idaho's retirement advantages.

Schedule Your Idaho Consultation

IDAHO FINANCIAL ADVISOR

Idaho Retirement Planning with a Trusted Fiduciary Advisor

Capital Wealth serves Idaho families with comprehensive retirement planning. As fiduciaries, we put your interests first — no product sales, no hidden commissions, just honest advice tailored to Idaho's unique tax and retirement landscape.

SCHEDULE YOUR VISIT →

THE PROBLEM

Does This Sound Familiar?

You need a fiduciary, not a salesperson

Most "advisors" in Idaho are insurance agents or brokers earning commissions. You need someone legally required to act in your best interest — a true fiduciary.

You need Idaho-specific tax planning

Idaho has a flat 5.8% income tax on retirement income. Your advisor should understand how this impacts withdrawal strategies, Roth conversions, and Social Security taxation.

You're planning cross-border retirement

Many Idaho retirees have connections to Utah or other states. You need an advisor who understands multi-state tax considerations and how to optimize across state lines.

You've saved but have no income plan

You've built retirement savings, but you don't have a clear strategy for creating reliable income, managing taxes, coordinating Social Security, and ensuring your money lasts.

THE SOLUTION

Fiduciary Planning for Idaho Families

Why Idaho Families Trust Capital Wealth

Based in Lehi, Utah, we serve Idaho families virtually and have extensive experience with Idaho's tax laws and retirement planning considerations. We understand Idaho's 5.8% flat income tax, property tax implications for retirees, and the absence of estate or inheritance taxes in the state.

Mike Stevens brings 25+ years of experience, a fiduciary commitment, and a track record of serving over 650 clients. We provide comprehensive retirement planning — income strategies, tax optimization, Social Security planning, and Medicare guidance — all with a fiduciary standard that puts your interests first.

Fiduciary Legally required to act in your best interest, not ours

Areas We Serve in Idaho:

  • Boise and the Treasure Valley
  • Meridian and surrounding communities
  • Nampa and Canyon County
  • Idaho Falls and Eastern Idaho
  • Pocatello and Southeastern Idaho
  • Twin Falls and South-Central Idaho
  • Coeur d'Alene and Northern Idaho
  • Virtual meetings available statewide

IDAHO TAX CONSIDERATIONS

Idaho Retirement Tax Planning

1

Flat 5.8% Income Tax

Idaho taxes retirement income at a flat 5.8% rate. This includes 401(k)/IRA withdrawals, pensions, and most other income. Strategic planning can help minimize this tax burden.

2

No Estate or Inheritance Tax

Idaho does not impose estate or inheritance taxes, which simplifies legacy planning. This can be advantageous for wealth transfer strategies compared to many other states.

3

Property Tax Planning

Idaho offers property tax relief programs for seniors, including the Circuit Breaker program. We help you understand and maximize available benefits.

COMMON QUESTIONS
